references.reason: A veranda table makes fan cues, shared servings, and protected conversational space visible.
Fan cue cards teach the difference between inviting a story, serving a course, and respecting a stopped boundary.
A shared tray makes Youmu's finite kitchen cadence and Yuyuko's hosting responsibility actionable.
The banquet handoff balances stories and courses with clear, assisted, and loss-continuing returns.
The opening observation records appetite as hosting intelligence rather than a single food-vacuum joke.
A quiet parlor gives a newly arrived spirit control over when a formal crossing notice returns to view.
A true but temporarily covered notice distinguishes gentle pacing from denial.
A movable screen makes protection revisable rather than a permanent decision about the spirit's mood.
The journal records levity that interrupts fear without trivializing death or stealing the next question.
A quiet veranda gives an ended meal room to remain empty without becoming an Archive reconstruction puzzle.
An empty plate carries the end of a meal without pretending to preserve its recipe or flavor.
An anonymous personal gesture keeps the shared person present without inventing a named canon relationship.
The journal records relational memory and an ending respected without forced reconstruction.
A ribbon walk can visibly hold bright festival welcome and memorial weight in the same space.
A crooked bright ribbon expresses chosen lightness without erasing whom the memorial carries.
A stopped fan makes Yuyuko's real boundary legible before levity resumes.
The journal preserves both the strain caused by imposed solemnity and Yuyuko's chosen lightness.
